posts.title: Training plan — pressing triggers & set pieces
posts.body:
## Session focus

Today we work on two key areas: **pressing triggers** and **corner routines**.

### Pressing

Press in pairs from the front. Trigger = GK pass to CB or slow build-up. Once triggered, the two forwards close immediately, midfield shifts to cut passing lanes.

### Set pieces

Corner routine A: near-post flick-on. Corner routine B: pull-back to the edge. Practice both 5 times each.

### Notes

- Focus on compactness when we don't have the ball
- Fast transition the moment we win it — no hesitation

posts.title: Post-match — 2–1 win vs AEK W
posts.body:
## What worked ✓

**High press in the first half** — we won the ball high up the pitch 4 times and converted 2. The pressing triggers were read well.

**Quick transitions** — as soon as we won the ball we moved fast. The striker's run behind the line was excellent.

## What to fix ✗

**Defensive shape in 2nd half** — after going 2–0 up we dropped too deep and let them play. We need to stay compact and keep pressing even when leading.

**Spacing** — the gap between midfield and defence grew too large after 60 min. We'll work on this in Tuesday's session.

posts.title: Pre-match briefing — vs ΜΠΑΛΙΤΣΑ
posts.body:
## Game plan

We press in a **4-3-3 high block**.

### Pressing triggers
- GK pass to CB → press immediately
- Slow build-up from their defence → all three forwards engage
- Their full-back receiving with back to goal → nearest winger closes

### Attacking

Exploit wide areas — their full-backs push high and leave space in behind. Our wingers should make runs in behind on every transition.

### Set pieces

- Corners: routine A (near-post)
- Free kicks in our half: long ball to the striker's run
